taskDescription: Click on the words that represent congenital anomalies or developmental defects of the head and neck region (AN43.4).
textField: The development of the head and neck involves the pharyngeal arches, each contributing specific structures. A *thyroglossal* cyst presents as a midline neck swelling that moves with tongue protrusion. A *branchial* cyst appears anterior to the sternocleidomastoid muscle as a lateral neck swelling. The most common congenital anomaly of the face is a *cleft* lip, resulting from failure of fusion of the maxillary and medial nasal processes. *Ectopic* thyroid tissue may be found along the path of descent from the foramen caecum. The pituitary gland develops from two sources and a *craniopharyngioma* may develop from remnants of Rathke's pouch. A child with *Pierre-Robin* sequence presents with micrognathia from first arch maldevelopment. Failure of the second pharyngeal membrane to resorb causes *atresia* of the external auditory meatus.
